meta.title: Motherland Lost: The Egyptian and Coptic Quest for Modernity
meta.description: In his new book, Motherland Lost: The Egyptian and Coptic Quest for Modernity (Hoover Institution), Hudson Institute Research Fellow Samuel Tadros argues that the modern plight of Copts is inseparable from Egypt's quest for modernity. As Egypt's intellectuals and state modernizers attempted to answer the challenges posed by the discovery of the West's advancement, Copts were faced with a similar crisis with the onslaught of foreign missionaries and the challenge of modernizing their ancient chur
